title: Malinithan
tagline: Malinithan — spiritual town of Arunachal Pradesh

overview.short: Malinithan is an archaeological site containing the ruins of an early medieval period temple on the northern bank of the Brahmaputra River in the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh.
overview.description: Malinithan is an archaeological site containing the ruins of an early medieval period temple on the northern bank of the Brahmaputra River in the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh. It is generally attributed to the Chutia kings in the 13th-14th century. Art-historical study of the iconography and development of Indian temple art suggests that the cultural period represented by the Malinithan remains extended from about the 10th to the 14th century. The scale of its stone architecture has also been taken to indicate construction under a prosperous polity before the Ahoms, with its latest phase

topPlaces.name: Likabali
topPlaces.description: Likabali, a town in the foothills, is the Headquarters of 22nd district i.e. Lower Siang district of Arunachal Pradesh state of India.

topPlaces.name: Kane Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Kane Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area and wildlife sanctuary located in West Siang district of the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh. The sanctuary covers an area of 31 km2 (12 sq mi) and was declared as a protected area in 1991.

topPlaces.name: Silapathar
topPlaces.description: Silapathar is a small city and municipal board in Dhemaji district in the Indian state of Assam. The name itself derived from Assamese word "Sila" means Kite and "Pothar" means rice fields as the place used be a kiting ground for native Mising people during the winters.

items.note: Likabali, a town in the foothills, is the Headquarters of 22nd district i.e. Lower Siang district of Arunachal Pradesh state of India.

Kane Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area and wildlife sanctuary located in West Siang district of the Indian state of Arunachal Pradesh. The sanctuary covers an area of 31 km2 (12 sq mi) and was declared as a protected area in 1991.

Silapathar is a small city and municipal board in Dhemaji district in the Indian state of Assam. The name itself derived from Assamese word "Sila" means Kite and "Pothar" means rice fields as the place used be a kiting ground for native Mising people during the winters.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Malinithan?
faq.a: The best time to visit Malinithan is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

The nearest airport is Dibrugarh Airport (36 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (217 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Malinithan?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Malinithan at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Malinithan?
faq.a: Stays in Malinithan start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Malinithan?
faq.a: The top attractions are Likabali, Kane Wildlife Sanctuary, Silapathar.
